course_title: Anatomy and Physiology for Nursing and Midwifery
course_description:
BIOM1060 provides nursing and midwifery students with an opportunity to develop an understanding of the interdependence of structure & function in humans at the cellular, tissues, organ, organ system and organism levels of function.

BIOM1060 is the first in series of biomedical science courses for Nursing and Midwifery students. In this course you will learn the foundations of biomedical science, with a focus on anatomy and physiology. The course begins with an introduction to human structure and function, and the basic principles of cell biology. You will then investigate the anatomy and physiology of the musculoskeletal, nervous, cardiovascular, respiratory, gastrointestinal, renal and reproductive systems. The knowledge and skills obtained in this course will allow you to understand aspects of normal human organ and body system structure and function, and aid in the subsequent recognition and understanding of abnormal function in disease and application of various treatment strategies for acute and chronic illnesses.

Practical classes conducted in the Gross Anatomy Facility (GAF) in the Otto Hirschfeld Building (#81), St Lucia Campus use human cadaveric specimens. Students unable to fully participate for medical, personal or religious reasons, should discuss their situation with the Course Coordinator prior to enrolment.

Of special note:

course_contacts.name: Student and Academic Administration Team
course_contacts.notes: The SBMS Student and Academic Administration Team is located on Level 1 of the Sir William Macgregor Building (64-130).

course_aims: Students undertaking BIOM1060 should understand and appreciate that:

learning_outcomes.description: describe the structure of the cells, tissues and organs that constitute some of the human organ systems

identify the principal components, relevant anatomical features and relationships of some of the human organ systems on human cadaveric specimens and communicate effectively using correct anatomical terminology
define the principal functions of human organ systems and summarise the relationships between the various organ systems

identify and explain the importance of homeostasis in human survival, including the importance of the cellular environment and the cell membrane, cellular communication, and integration of cellular responses

describe the link between human biology and disease processes and predict some functional deficits that may occur as a result of altered structure, within an ethical framework of respect for both living and deceased humans
explain how anatomical and physiological changes occur throughout the life cycle, including pregnancy, embryology, and foetal development
work scientifically, propose explanations, test hypotheses and draw conclusions about human anatomy and physiology through practical experimentation
demonstrate the importance of physiological tests used in health care settings
reflect effectively on your learning in biomedical science.

assessment_summary.title: In-person engagement worksheets
assessment_summary.due_date: Worksheet 1: Cell and nervous system physiology tutorial.; Worksheet 2: Nervous system and Musculoskeletal anatomy tutorial.; Worksheet 3: Musculoskeletal anatomy practical.; Worksheet 4: Electromyography practical.; Worksheet 5: Spirometry practical.; Worksheet 6: Reproductive anatomy practical.; Worksheet 7: Respiratory and reproductive system tutorial.; Worksheet 8: Gastrointestinal anatomy practical.; Worksheet 9: Learning workshop.; Worksheets are due during class. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend.

assessment_details.title: In-person engagement worksheets
assessment_details.due_date: Worksheet 1: Cell and nervous system physiology tutorial. Worksheet 2: Nervous system and Musculoskeletal anatomy tutorial. Worksheet 3: Musculoskeletal anatomy practical. Worksheet 4: Electromyography practical. Worksheet 5: Spirometry practical. Worksheet 6: Reproductive anatomy practical. Worksheet 7: Respiratory and reproductive system tutorial. Worksheet 8: Gastrointestinal anatomy practical. Worksheet 9: Learning workshop. Worksheets are due during class. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend.
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment involves completing a worksheet during your five (5) practicals and four (4) tutorials. There will be nine (9) opportunities to complete worksheets in BIOM1060. Each worksheet is worth 4%. Your best marks for six (6) worksheets will count towards 24%. Worksheet questions will consist primarily of short answer questions. Please note that worksheets must be completed in class. Further information about the exam will be provided on Blackboard. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
A request for an extension must be submitted through my.UQ as soon as it becomes evident that an extension is needed, but no later than one calendar day after the assessment task submission due date and time. Include the name of the worksheet as per the Assessment Section of the Course Profile in your extension application.
Requests for extension received more than one calendar day after the assessment task submission due date and time will not be accepted for consideration unless you provide evidence of exceptional circumstances preventing you from submitting a request prior to the deadline, e.g., due to hospitalisation.
If you have been granted an extension, then the assessment specified penalty listed under Late Submission will be applied to submissions made after the due date of the approved extension.
Late submission
No late submissions possible.

assessment_details.title: In-semester exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This exam will include a variety of questions, including but not limited to short answer and multiple-choice questions. Details about the content of the exam (including topics) and the examination format will be provided on Blackboard during the semester. The exam will be a closed book invigilated exam held on campus, undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform. Students will be required to bring a laptop to the exam that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library. The Library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera. Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .
learning_resources.other_course_materials: If we've listed something under further requirement, you'll need to provide your own. Required Item Description Further Requirement Textbook Students enrolled in BIOM1060 will be given access to Mastering A&P (an online quizzing platform) and the associated e-textbook, Marieb and Hoehn (2019), Human Anatomy & Physiology. Please note that you do not have to purchase these resources unless you would like a paper version of the textbook. Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) & Identification All students attending the Gross Anatomy Facility (GAF) must wear the following Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) & Identification at all times: Clean white laboratory coat, safety glasses and fully enclosed shoes. Students will not be permitted in the laboratory without these items. Each student must have their UQ Student Identification Card visibly displayed above the waist. While public health directions no longer require face masks to be worn, they are recommended when you cannot physically distance, or are a vulnerable person or caring for vulnerable people. Please note cloth masks are not acceptable in laboratory classes in the School of Biomedical Sciences. PPE and identification card holders can be purchased from the School Locker. own item needed Online Anatomy Induction Training Students must complete the online training for the "SBMS Code of Conduct for use of Donated Tissues" and "Working Safely in the GAF for students" at the UQ Blackboard training website (Biomedical Sciences Student Training). Completion requires 100% correct answers to the test and a declaration for each of the two training modules. Both training modules must be completed 72 hours prior to attending the GAF and at least once in each calendar year. A record of student completions of this training will be cross-checked against class rolls for each practical attendance. Students will not be permitted entry to the GAF if these induction modules have not been completed in full. Face-to-Face GAF Induction At the first scheduled GAF practical, students are required to undertake face-to-face induction. A record of student completion of this training will be cross-checked against class rolls for each practical attendance. Students will not be permitted entry to the GAF if these induction modules have not been completed in full. Transplantation and Anatomy Act, 1979 (Qld) http://www.legislation.qld.gov.au/browse/inforce Criminal Code Act, 1899 (Qld) http://www.legislation.qld.gov.au/browse/inforce Anatomy Facilities and Programs Governance Policy https://policies.uq.edu.au/document/view-current.php?id=122 SBMS Code of Conduct for Use of Donated Human Tissues http://www.biomedical-sciences.uq.edu.au/files/12049/SBMS_CodeOfConduct_forHumanTissueUse_2016_1_HRECB.pdf

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 2 (23 Feb - 08 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Module 1: Cells [Ainscough] In this module, you will learn about: The fundamentals of A&P Cell structure and function Membrane transport This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 2.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 3 (23 Feb - 15 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Module 2: The nervous system [Ainscough and Ngo] In this module, you will learn about: Introduction to the nervous system and resting membrane potentials (Ainscough); Changes in membrane potentials (Ainscough); Neural communication (Ainscough); The autonomic nervous system (Ainscough); Anatomy of the central nervous system (Ngo); Anatomy of the peripheral nervous system (Ngo); Reflexes (Ngo). This content will be covered in revision lectures in week 2 and 4.
GAF induction Practicals will run in the GAF. This induction is compulsory before attending anatomy practicals.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 4 (09 Mar - 22 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Module 3: Musculoskeletal system [Ngo] In this module, you will learn about: The skeletal system; Joints; The muscular system. This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 4.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 13 (09 Mar - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Tutorial 1, 2, 3 and 4 Tutorial 1 - Cell and nervous system physiolog y tutorial. In this session, you will revise concepts from the cell module and nervous system physiology. Week 3 (9 Mar - 13 Mar) Tutorial 2 - Nervous system and musculoskeletal anatomy tutorial. In this session, you will revise concepts from the anatomy of the nervous system and the musculoskeletal system. Week 4 (16 Mar - 20 Mar) Tutorial 3 - Respiratory and reproductive system tutorial. In this session, you will revise concepts from the respiratory and reproductive systems. This practical will run in weeks 8 and 12. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend. From Week 8 To Week 12 (20 Apr - 22 May) Tutorial 4 - Learning workshop tutorial. In this session, you will discover evidence-based approaches to effective learning in preparation for the end of semester exam. This tutorial will run in weeks 9 and 13. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend . From Week 9 To Week 13 (27 Apr - 29 May)

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 4 To Week 5 (16 Mar - 29 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Module 4: Cardiovascular system [Cuffe and Faber] In this module, you will learn about: Cardiovascular anatomy (Cuffe); The heart (Faber); ECG (Faber); Blood pressure (Faber). This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 5.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 4 To Week 13 (16 Mar - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Practical 1,2,3,4 and 5 Musculoskeletal anatomy (Practical)- This is an anatomy practical, which will run in the GAF . Week 5 (23 Mar - 27 Mar). Electrocardiography (Practical)- This is a physiology practical, which will run in the MacGregor Laboratories . Week 6 (30 Mar - 03 Apr). Spirometry (Practical)- This is a physiology practical, which will run in the MacGregor Laboratories. This practical will run in weeks 7 and 10. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend. From Week 7 To Week 10 (13 Apr - 08 May). Reproductive anatomy (Practical)- This is an anatomy practical, which will run in the GAF. This practical will run in weeks 8 and 13. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend . From Week 8 To Week 13 (20 Apr - 29 May). Gastrointestinal anatomy (Practical)- This is an anatomy practical, which will run in the GAF . This practical will run in weeks 11 and 12. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend . From Week 11 To Week 12 (11 May - 22 May).
Module 5: Respiratory system [Suen] In this module, you will learn about: Respiratory anatomy; Gas exchange and transport; Respiratory mechanics and lung volumes; Control of respiration.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 7 To Week 12 (13 Apr - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic: Module 6: Reproductive system [Cuffe] Please note that these lectures will be pre-recorded and uploaded to blackboard. You should view these lectures before your associated practical or tutorial. In this module, you will learn about: Conception, embryology and foetal development; Male reproductive system; Female reproductive system; Reproductive physiology. This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 8 or 12.
Module 7: Gastrointestinal system [Cuffe] In this module, you will learn about: The upper GIT; Accessory organs; The lower GIT. This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 9.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 10 To Week 13 (04 May - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Module 8: Renal system [Rajapaske] Please note that these lectures will be pre-recorded and uploaded to blackboard. You should view these lectures before your associated practical or tutorial. In this module, you will learn about: Renal anatomy; Glomerular filtration; Filtration control, Reabsorption and secretion. This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 10 or 13.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . School guidelines Your school has additional guidelines you'll need to follow for this course: SBMS Assessment Guidelines Course guidelines State Legislation Undergraduate and Postgraduate Students should be familiar with and shall not contravene: The Transplantation and Anatomy Act, 1979 (Qld) - http://www.legislation.qld.gov.au/LEGISLTN/CURRENT/T/TransplAAnatA79.pdf The Criminal Code Act, 1899 (Qld) - https://www.legislation.qld.gov.au/legisltn/current/c/crimincode.pdf Learning activities
